question: The probability of cat ownership $\operatorname{Pr}(A)=0.35$. The probability of dog ownership given cat ownership $\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid A)=0.4286$..
answer: By the general rule for multiplication, the probability of owning a cat and a $\operatorname{dog} \operatorname{Pr}(A$ and $B)= \operatorname{Pr}(A) 	imes \operatorname{Pr}(B \mid A)=0.35 	imes 0.4286=0.15$

meta info.caption: General rule for multiplication (Pet ownership example).
meta info.section: 5.5  MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
